originalAlarm.description: Dereference of null pointer (loaded from variable 'ops')

presenceCondition:
(or (and CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN (not CONFIG_OF_IRQ))
    (and CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE (not CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN) (not CONFIG_OF_IRQ)))
model:
(define-fun CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE () Bool
  true)
(define-fun CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN () Bool
  false)
(define-fun CONFIG_OF_IRQ () Bool
  false)
reasoning: 6252547b8a7.c L12: ops null pointer dereference (// ERROR). In twl_probe(), ops=NULL when !CONFIG_OF_IRQ; irq_domain_add(ops) then dereferences *ops at L12. PC: (TWL4030_CORE AND IRQ_DOMAIN AND !OF_IRQ) OR (TWL4030_CORE AND !IRQ_DOMAIN AND !OF_IRQ). The second branch is contradictory: CONFIG_TWL4030_CORE=T implies CONFIG_IRQ_DOMAIN (derived macro, L3-5 of source), so TWL4030_CORE=T AND !IRQ_DOMAIN is non-compilable. PC is over-constrained by including this contradictory configuration. Known bug.
